questions.question: Formula for work done when force is at an angle θ with displacement
questions.explanation: Only the component of force along displacement does work: W = F × d × cosθ.

questions.question: Power is defined as
questions.explanation: Power = Work / Time; SI unit is Watt (1 J/s).

questions.question: A moving object stops; what happens to its kinetic energy?
questions.explanation: KE = 0 when object stops; work done by friction converts KE to other forms.

questions.question: If work done is negative, it means
questions.explanation: Negative work occurs when the force is opposite to the displacement.

questions.question: Work done by friction is usually
questions.explanation: Friction opposes motion → negative work.

questions.question: Work done by gravity on a freely falling body is
questions.explanation: Gravity assists motion → positive work.

questions.question: Which of the following is true about mechanical work?

options.A: Work can be done without displacement
options.D: Work does not depend on force

questions.explanation: Work can be negative if force opposes displacement.

questions.question: If a force acts on an object but there is no displacement, work done is
questions.explanation: Work = F × d × cosθ; if displacement d = 0 → Work = 0.

questions.question: A 2 kg object moves with 3 m/s. Its kinetic energy is
A body is lifted to a height h. Work done against gravity is
questions.explanation: Work done against gravity = Potential energy gained = m × g × h.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of work done?

options.A: Holding a heavy bag without moving

questions.explanation: Work is done only if object is displaced by applied force.

questions.question: Mechanical energy of a body is the sum of
questions.explanation: Mechanical energy = KE + PE.

questions.question: Work done by a conservative force depends on
questions.explanation: Conservative forces depend only on initial and final positions (e.g., gravity, spring).

questions.question: Rate at which work is done is called
questions.explanation: Power = Work / Time; measured in Watt (W).

questions.question: Negative work occurs when

options.A: Force is in the same direction as displacement

questions.explanation: If force acts opposite to displacement, work is negative.

questions.question: Unit of power is
questions.explanation: Power is measured in watts (W).

questions.question: If a body moves horizontally under a vertical force, work done by the force is
questions.explanation: Force perpendicular to displacement does no work.

questions.question: Energy can be transformed from one form to another. Example is

options.A: Falling body converting PE to KE

questions.explanation: PE lost = KE gained; energy transformation occurs.

questions.question: A spring stores energy when stretched. This energy is
questions.explanation: Energy stored in stretched spring = potential energy.

questions.question: Work done by a force along displacement is positive if

options.A: Force and displacement are in same direction

questions.explanation: Work is positive when force has a component in the direction of displacement.

questions.question: Which of these is an example of mechanical work done?
questions.explanation: Mechanical work requires displacement along the force applied.

questions.question: Power is calculated as
questions.explanation: Power = Work / Time; measured in watts.

questions.question: Kinetic energy formula is
questions.explanation: KE = 1/2 × mass × velocity².

questions.question: Potential energy depends on
questions.explanation: PE = m × g × h.

questions.question: Work done = 0 when
questions.explanation: If displacement is zero or force is perpendicular, work done = 0.
